key_highlights: Strait of Hormuz crisis is the biggest energy disruption ever, with traffic near zero and a billion-barrel oil shock expected to crash demand.
US-Iran talks appear unlikely to reach a breakthrough, with the White House sending a team to Pakistan amid the standoff.
Intel stock surges 23% after earnings, on track for record close, driven by AI optimism.
Nvidia stock clinches record close, pushing market cap over $5 trillion again.
Google investing up to $40 billion in Anthropic, signaling massive AI infrastructure buildout.
DOJ drops probe of Fed Chair Powell, smoothing path for Kevin Warsh and reducing political uncertainty.
Bitcoin stealth rally has traders targeting $80,000, with VanEck seeing more potential gains.
Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders approve Paramount Skydance merger, reshaping media landscape.
Big Tech job cuts and AI-driven upheaval in Irish tech jobs signal structural shifts.
EU leaders convene in Cyprus amid Iran war, highlighting geopolitical escalation.

sectors.trend: Extreme disruption due to Strait of Hormuz blockade; oil supply shock is imminent, with demand destruction expected.
sectors.implications: Energy prices likely to spike sharply, benefiting US oil producers and alternative energy, but hurting import-dependent economies and airlines. Shipping waivers provide temporary relief but uncertainty remains high.

sectors.trend: Strong momentum driven by AI investments and earnings beats; Intel and Nvidia lead. AI infrastructure spending continues to accelerate with Google's $40B Anthropic investment.
sectors.implications: AI-related stocks remain a core growth theme. Semiconductor demand is robust, but job cuts at Big Tech and AI-driven automation pose risks to labor markets and valuations.

sectors.trend: Mixed; Berkshire Hathaway highlighted as best holding company stock. Jane Street posts record trading haul. DOJ dropping Powell probe reduces regulatory overhang.
sectors.implications: Financial sector benefits from reduced political risk and strong trading volumes. Holding companies with diversified portfolios offer defensive qualities amid geopolitical turmoil.

sectors.trend: Consolidation continues with Warner Bros. Discovery/Paramount Skydance merger approved. TKO Group expands UFC into new markets.
sectors.implications: Media sector is restructuring; scale and content libraries become key. Live sports and events remain valuable assets.

sectors.trend: Polestar reports 34% retail sales growth; BYD's rise continues. Honda exits South Korea. EV charging infrastructure expands with ChargePoint launch.
sectors.implications: EV adoption is uneven; winners like BYD and Polestar show growth, but legacy automakers face margin pressure. Infrastructure buildout supports long-term EV thesis.

sectors.trend: PepsiCo named best slow-growth stock; Constellation Brands upgraded. Starbucks invests $100M in Nashville despite boycott calls.
sectors.implications: Defensive consumer staples remain attractive in uncertain macro environment. Brand strength and pricing power are key.

sectors.trend: Spirit Airlines faces potential bailout; 12 trucking firms file for bankruptcy. Shipping waiver extended 90 days for energy flows.
sectors.implications: Transportation sector under severe stress from fuel costs and demand shifts. Bailout discussions indicate systemic risk.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin rallies toward $80,000; VanEck sees further gains. AI crypto tokens gain attention. Kalshi fines politicians for insider trading.
sectors.implications: Crypto markets are decoupling from traditional risk assets, driven by institutional adoption and geopolitical hedging. Regulatory scrutiny remains a risk.

sectors.trend: Iran crisis dominates; EU leaders meet in Cyprus. US sanctions Chinese refiner Hengli over Iran links. Russian attacks on Ukraine continue.
sectors.implications: Defense spending likely to increase. Sanctions and supply chain disruptions will affect global trade. Energy security is paramount.

key_themes: Strait of Hormuz blockade causing unprecedented energy disruption
AI investment boom driving tech sector outperformance
Geopolitical risk premium rising across assets
Central bank and regulatory uncertainty (DOJ probe dropped, Fed policy)
Corporate earnings season showing divergence between tech and traditional sectors
Crypto rally as alternative store of value
Labor market shifts due to AI automation

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Strait of Hormuz blockade / Iran conflict escalation
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to US energy producers, alternative energy, and defense stocks. Reduce exposure to oil-importing sectors (airlines, shipping). Hedge with commodities and inflation-protected securities.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI-driven job displacement and regulatory backlash
risk_assessment.mitigation: Invest in AI infrastructure and automation beneficiaries, but avoid overvalued pure plays. Monitor regulatory developments in EU and US. Diversify across sectors less exposed to automation.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Inflation resurgence due to energy shock
risk_assessment.mitigation: Allocate to TIPS, commodities, and value stocks. Reduce duration in fixed income. Consider floating rate bonds.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Market volatility from political and trade policy uncertainty
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain cash reserves and use options strategies for tail risk hedging. Focus on high-quality, low-beta stocks. Monitor Trump administration actions and Fed signals.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Corporate credit stress in transportation and airlines
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid high-yield bonds in vulnerable sectors. Favor investment-grade credits. Monitor Spirit Airlines bailout outcome as bellwether.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech sector valuation bubble in AI-related stocks
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on high-flying AI names. Rotate into diversified tech ETFs or value-oriented tech. Use trailing stops.
Reduce exposure to Eastern European markets. Increase defense and energy holdings. Monitor grain and commodity supply chains.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to US energy producers and oilfield services
opportunities.rationale: The Hormuz blockade will drive oil prices higher, benefiting domestic producers. Companies with US-centric production are less exposed to geopolitical risk.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI infrastructure and semiconductor leaders on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: Massive capex from Google, Amazon, and others into AI ensures sustained demand for chips and data centers. Intel's earnings beat and Nvidia's record close confirm momentum.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Bitcoin and select crypto assets as geopolitical hedge
opportunities.rationale: Bitcoin's stealth rally toward $80,000 amid geopolitical turmoil suggests it is being used as a store of value. VanEck's positive outlook and institutional adoption support further gains.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in defense and cybersecurity stocks
opportunities.rationale: Escalating Iran conflict and global instability will drive increased defense budgets. Cybersecurity is critical amid AI hacking threats.

opportunities.recommendation: Add to consumer staples and defensive dividend stocks
opportunities.rationale: In a volatile macro environment, companies like PepsiCo, Constellation Brands, and Evergy offer stable earnings and dividends. Wells Fargo's price target increases support this view.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to oil-importing sectors (airlines, shipping, heavy manufacturing)
defensive_moves.rationale: The Hormuz crisis will spike fuel costs and disrupt supply chains, hurting margins in these sectors. Spirit Airlines' bailout talks highlight the stress.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge portfolio with gold, TIPS, and volatility products
defensive_moves.rationale: Inflation risk from energy shock and geopolitical uncertainty warrant inflation hedges and tail-risk protection. Gold and TIPS provide diversification.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Trim overvalued AI and tech momentum names
defensive_moves.rationale: While AI theme is strong, valuations are stretched. Profit-taking on names with high P/E ratios reduces downside risk if sentiment shifts.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation to 10-15% of portfolio
defensive_moves.rationale: Elevated geopolitical and market uncertainty warrants liquidity to take advantage of potential dislocations. Cash provides optionality.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets will remain highly volatile due to the Strait of Hormuz crisis and US-Iran tensions. Energy stocks and AI tech will outperform, while transportation and consumer discretionary face headwinds. Bitcoin may continue its rally as a geopolitical hedge. Earnings season will drive individual stock moves, with tech earnings likely to beat expectations. The DOJ dropping the Powell probe reduces political risk for financials.
market_outlook.long_term: The AI investment cycle will sustain tech leadership, but energy disruption could trigger a recession if oil prices stay elevated. Central banks may face a stagflationary dilemma. The shift toward AI automation will reshape labor markets and create winners and losers across sectors. Geopolitical fragmentation will increase defense spending and reshoring. Crypto adoption may accelerate as a non-sovereign store of value. Holding companies like Berkshire Hathaway offer resilience.
key_catalysts: Resolution or escalation of US-Iran talks and Hormuz blockade
Fed policy response to inflation from energy shock
Big Tech earnings (Apple, Amazon, Google) next week
Spirit Airlines bailout decision and broader airline sector stress
AI regulation developments in US and EU
Warner Bros. Discovery/Paramount Skydance merger closing
SpaceX IPO potential impact on liquidity
China EV maker BYD's continued expansion
EU leaders' summit outcomes on Iran and energy security
watch_list: Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic and oil prices (WTI, Brent)
Fed speeches and FOMC minutes for inflation stance
AI-related earnings and capex guidance from tech giants
Credit spreads and high-yield bond market stress
Jobless claims and employment data for AI displacement effects
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
